title: 10 Best Medicinal Preparations of Paeonia lactiflora (White Peony)

preparations.constituent_desc: Paeonia Lactiflora infusion is a medicinal preparation made by steeping dried root or rhizome of the white peony plant in hot water, which releases the plant's beneficial compounds into a liquid form. This infusion is known to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting relaxation. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried root or rhizome of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. To make a Paeonia lactiflora infusion, pour boiling water over the dried root or rhizome and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. It is important to strain the liquid before consuming. When using this Paeonia lactiflora preparation, it is recommended to start with a low dose and increase gradually, as it can cause digestive upset in some individuals. Additionally, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using this infusion if you are taking any medications or have any pre-existing medical conditions.

Paeonia Lactiflora tincture is a medicinal preparation made by soaking the dried root or underground stem (rhizome) of the white peony plant in alcohol or another solvent to extract its active compounds. The main health benefits of this tincture include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and headaches, among other conditions. The root or rhizome of the Paeonia lactiflora plant is used to make the tincture, and it contains various bioactive compounds such as paeoniflorin, which is believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. To make the tincture, the dried and chopped root or rhizome is soaked in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, for several weeks, and then the liquid is filtered and stored in a cool, dark place. When using Paeonia Lactiflora tincture, it is essential to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider, as it can interact with certain medications and have side effects such as digestive upset or skin irritation. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using this tincture, as its safety during pregnancy and lactation has not been established.

Paeonia Lactiflora decoction is a traditional medicinal preparation made by boiling the roots of the white peony plant in water. The main health benefits of this preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and sedative effects, making it useful for treating various conditions such as menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and insomnia. The dried or fresh roots of the Paeonia lactiflora plant are the main plant parts used for this preparation. To make the decoction, place the peony roots in a pot and add cold water, then bring to a boil and simmer for about 30 minutes. After straining, the resulting liquid can be consumed. It is important to note that this preparation should be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with certain medications and may have side effects for individuals with certain medical conditions.

Paeonia Lactiflora fluid extract is a preparation made by extracting the medicinal properties of the Paeonia lactiflora plant using a liquid solvent, resulting in a concentrated, alcohol-based extract. The main health benefits of this preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and sedative effects, making it useful for treating conditions such as menstrual cramps, arthritis, and nervous tension. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried root and rhizome of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. To make the fluid extract, the root and rhizome are chopped and soaked in a solvent such as ethanol or methanol, then pressed to extract the liquid. When using this Paeonia lactiflora preparation, it is important to follow the recommended dosage, as excessive use can cause digestive upset. Additionally, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using this preparation if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking other medications, as it can interact with certain drugs.

Paeonia Lactiflora dry extract is a concentrated form of the White Peony plant, created by extracting and removing the volatile components, resulting in a dry, powdered substance. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, regulating menstrual cycles, and improving liver function. The main plant parts used are the roots of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. To make this preparation, the roots are first cleaned, chopped, and dried before being extracted with a solvent. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using this Paeonia lactiflora preparation, as it can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for individuals with specific health conditions. Additionally, it should be taken in appropriate dosages, as directed by a healthcare provider, to avoid potential adverse effects.

Paeonia Lactiflora powder is a type of medicinal preparation made from the roots of the White Peony plant, a perennial flowering plant native to China. The main health benefits of this preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and sedative properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and nervous tension. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried and powdered roots of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. To make Paeonia Lactiflora powder, the roots are first carefully washed and dried, then ground into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a mechanical grinder. When using Paeonia Lactiflora powder, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider, as overuse can cause side effects such as digestive upset and dizziness. Additionally, it is not recommended for use during pregnancy or while breastfeeding, as it may have hormonal effects.

Paeonia Lactiflora capsules are a type of medicinal preparation made from the dried root of the White Peony plant, a perennial flowering plant native to China. The main health benefits of this preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and various types of pain. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried root of the White Peony plant. To make Paeonia Lactiflora capsules, the dried root is typically ground into a powder and encapsulated. When using Paeonia Lactiflora capsules, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and to consult with a healthcare provider if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking other medications, as it may interact with certain drugs and may not be suitable for individuals with certain health conditions.

Paeonia Lactiflora tablets are a type of medicinal preparation made from the roots of the White Peony plant, a perennial flowering plant native to China. The main health benefits of this preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and joint pain. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried or fresh root of the White Peony plant. To make Paeonia Lactiflora tablets, the root of the White Peony plant is first cleaned, dried, and then ground into a fine powder. This powder is then mixed with a binder and compressed into tablets. When using Paeonia Lactiflora tablets, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and not to exceed it, as overuse can lead to digestive side effects such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. Additionally, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using this preparation if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have a medical condition.

Paeonia Lactiflora ointment is a topical preparation made from the roots of the White Peony plant, also known as Paeonia lactiflora. This ointment has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic properties. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the dried or fresh root of the Paeonia lactiflora plant. To make the ointment, the roots are first cleaned, chopped, and then boiled in water. After boiling, the liquid is strained, and the resulting extract is mixed with a base such as beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth, easy-to-spread ointment. When using Paeonia Lactiflora ointment, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and avoid applying it to broken or irritated skin. Additionally, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using this preparation, especially for children, pregnant women, and individuals with underlying health conditions.

intro: Paeonia lactiflora, also known as the Chinese peony, is a plant that has been used in medicinal preparations for centuries. One common preparation is a tisane, or herbal tea, made by steeping the dried or fresh root in hot water. This tisane is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome. Another preparation is a tincture, which is made by soaking the root in alcohol to extract its active compounds. The tincture can be taken orally and is thought to have similar benefits to the tisane, as well as potential additional benefits such as pain relief and immune system support. However, it is important to note that the effectiveness of these preparations and their potential side effects have not been thoroughly studied and more research is needed. Additionally, it is always recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new medicinal regimen.
